metadata.from: Bob (Robert) Hanifen — signed 'Bob', return address on envelope reads 'B.R. Bobhanifen 1706[?]00, Sqd. T-B55-279, Chanute Fld. Ill.'
metadata.to: James Edward Hanifen Sr. (Dad) — addressed to 'Hanifen Body & Paint Co., 964 Keo Way, Des Moines, Iowa'
metadata.location_sent_to: Hanifen Body & Paint Co., 964 Keo Way, Des Moines, Iowa
metadata.envelope_notes: Return address in upper left: 'B.R. Bobhanifen 1706[?]00, Sqd. T-B55-279, Chanute Fld. Ill.' Stamp area upper right shows '1/2' (half-cent or similar marking). Envelope is worn and aged.
topics: going to the show with a buddy
metadata.mood_tone: Casual, upbeat, and chatty; Bob is in good spirits, sharing updates about training, social life, and plans to come home.
metadata.legibility_notes: Some words are difficult to read due to cursive style and age of document. Page 2 has a large inked-out/redacted section obscuring one or two lines. The return address on the envelope is partially unclear. 'Chic' on page 1 may refer to Chicago or a nickname for a person/place. 'Willmette' could be 'Wilmette' (a Chicago suburb).
metadata.historical_context: Letter written in July 1945 during WWII. Bob is stationed at Chanute Field, Illinois, a major U.S. Army Air Forces training base. He is in school for riveting, suggesting aircraft maintenance training. The war in the Pacific was still ongoing at this time (V-J Day would come in August 1945).

pages.transcription:
Return address (upper left):
B.R. Bobhanifen 1706[?]00
Sqd. T-B55-279
Chanute Fld. Ill.

Postmark: RANTOUL, ILL. / JUL 20 / 11-PM / 1945

Addressed to:
Hanifen Body & Paint Co.
964 Keo Way
Des Moines, Iowa

Fri. 20

[U.S. ARMY AIR FORCES letterhead with bomber illustration]

Dear Dad,

  I received your letter today. Hey I don't work in the garden anymore; that was just temporary work while we were waiting to get in school. We don't do nothing now but go to school. We started riveting last night and I hit my finger twice. Boy was I mad at that hammer. I am going to go up to Chic[ago?] again this week.

  I will come home Aug. 4 if I can make it. The Sat. after Pay day. I have a chance to go see Willmette & Mark so I think I will try & make it over one of these week. A kid drives to Toledo every week in and a guy can ride over

for a buck or two. When I come home I will ride to Iowa City with some guy & hitch hike from there. Or else a kid who lives in Olen [Olin?] you up by Fort Dodge drives home every once in a while & I can ride clear thru with him. I went swimming this morning and the water was swell. I have to go see the Dentist Mon. or Tues. & get that other wisdom tooth pulled, only hope it is well by the time I get ready to come home.

[one or two lines redacted/blacked out]

Well Dad, toodley for now. I am going to the show with a buddie. It is the only time we can during the week.

Your Loving Son,
  Bob

P.S. Have you receive my bonds yet. I should have three $18.75 just checking up.

full_transcription:
Fri. 20

[U.S. ARMY AIR FORCES letterhead with bomber illustration]

Dear Dad,

  I received your letter today. Hey I don't work in the garden anymore; that was just temporary work while we were waiting to get in school. We don't do nothing now but go to school. We started riveting last night and I hit my finger twice. Boy was I mad at that hammer. I am going to go up to Chic[ago?] again this week.

  I will come home Aug. 4 if I can make it. The Sat. after Pay day. I have a chance to go see Willmette & Mark so I think I will try & make it over one of these week. A kid drives to Toledo every week in and a guy can ride over for a buck or two. When I come home I will ride to Iowa City with some guy & hitch hike from there. Or else a kid who lives in Olen [Olin?] you up by Fort Dodge drives home every once in a while & I can ride clear thru with him. I went swimming this morning and the water was swell. I have to go see the Dentist Mon. or Tues. & get that other wisdom tooth pulled, only hope it is well by the time I get ready to come home.

[one or two lines redacted/blacked out]

Well Dad, toodley for now. I am going to the show with a buddie. It is the only time we can during the week.

Your Loving Son,
  Bob

P.S. Have you receive my bonds yet. I should have three $18.75 just checking up.
summary: Written on July 20, 1945, from Chanute Field, Illinois, Bob writes to his father updating him on his military training, noting he has moved on from garden work to school, where he has begun riveting classes (and already hit his finger with a hammer). He discusses plans to come home on August 4th — the Saturday after payday — and outlines various hitchhiking and ride-sharing options for the trip back to Des Moines. He also mentions a pending dentist appointment to have a wisdom tooth pulled, a trip to see friends Willmette and Mark, and closes with a postscript asking whether his father has received his bonds, noting he should have three at $18.75 each.
